questions.questionText: Which is a postiive sign of pregnancy?
options: Fetal heart tones auscultated by Doppler
questions.correctAnswer: Fetal heart tones auscultated by Doppler
questions.explanationText: Amenorrhea is a presumptive sign. Positive pregnancy test is a probable sign. Chadwick sign is a probable indication (bluish-violet discoloration of the cervix, vagina).

questions.questionText: Pregnant client. What should nurse teach about nonstress testing?
options: This test is an invasive procedure
If the test is reactive, baby's heart rate is healthy.
The test should record the baby's heart rate decreases.
questions.correctAnswer: If the test is reactive, baby's heart rate is healthy.
questions.explanationText: Nonstress testing is noninvasive. A reactive test indicates fetal HR accelerates with fetal movement by about 15 beats/min.

questions.questionText: Client is 30 weeks of gestation and has gestational HTN.  Which indicates client needs biophysical profile?
questions.explanationText: Nonreactive non-stress test will extend duration of test with vibroacoustic stimulation to try to elicit a responde from fetus.  If still nonreactive, client should undergo biophysical profile (ultrasound assessment).

questions.questionText: Client is in prolonged labor. Which indicates fetal compromise?
options: Baseline fetal HR of 110 to 130 per minute

questions.questionText: Client has trichomoniasis and is prescribed metronidazole. What should the nurse teach?
options: You and your partner need to take the medication and use a condome during intercourse until cultures are negative.
questions.correctAnswer: You and your partner need to take the medication and use a condome during intercourse until cultures are negative.
questions.explanationText: Trichomonas vaginalis is an organism that causes STI.  Symptoms are yellowish to greenish, frothy, mucopurulent, copious discharge with unpleasant odor, itching, burning, or redness of vulva and vagina.

questions.questionText: Pregnant client is Rh-negative. In which situation should the nurse administer Rh(D) immune globuline?
options: Following an episode of influenza during pregnancy
questions.explanationText: Rh(D) immune globuline consist of passive antibodies against Rh factor, which will destroy any fetal RBCs in the maternal circulation and block maternal antibody production

questions.questionText: Newborn weighs 4lb.  How many kg is this?
Postpartum client reports abdominal cramping. What should nurse do?
options: Teach client to lie on her side
Encourage client to interact with newborn
questions.correctAnswer: Encourage client to interact with newborn
questions.explanationText: Baby interactions distract and decrease discomfort from uterine contractions. Client should lie prone to reduce discomfort. Ibuprofen or acetaminophen is recommended, not an opioid. Sitz bath is for perineal discomfort.

questions.questionText: Pre-eclampsia client is scheduled magnesium sulfate via continuous IV.  Which adverse effects should the nurse teach?
questions.explanationText: If magnesium sulfate, then client will feel sedated, lower BP, and feeling of warmth. Generalized pruritus can be an allergic reaction.

questions.questionText: Client is in active labor. Nurse notes late decelerations on fetal monitor. What should nurse do?
options: Apply oxygen at 10 L/min via nonrebreather face mask
questions.correctAnswer: Apply oxygen at 10 L/min via nonrebreather face mask
questions.explanationText: Not enough oxygen to fetus is uteroplacental insufficiency. Methyl-prostaglandin induces abortion or is for postpartum hemorrhage by delivering strong uterine contractions.

questions.questionText: Client is post-term labor.  Which is priority?
options: I had blood-streaked discharge a few hours ago
When my water broke, it was not clear
I have not felt my baby move as much today
I feel like I cannot breathe when I walk up the stairs
questions.correctAnswer: When my water broke, it was not clear
questions.explanationText: This is an injury risk to the newborn from meconium aspiration

questions.questionText: Newborn born 2 hours ago.  Which is respiratory distress?
questions.explanationText: Grunting indicates narrowing of bronchi.  Expected respiratory range is 30 to 60/min.

questions.questionText: Which should be taught to parents about bottle feeding?
options: Discard unused refrigerated formula after 72 hours
Boil water for powdered formula for 1-2 min
questions.correctAnswer: Boil water for powdered formula for 1-2 min
questions.explanationText: Parents should run tap water for 2 minutes, then boil for 1-2 minutes before mixing it with the formula to decrease contamination.

questions.questionText: Clinet is postpartum, breastfeeding, and has perineal hematoma.  Which breastfeeding position is recommended?
questions.explanationText: Clutch (or football) hold is good for cesarean birth.  Across-the-lap is for smaller babies. Cross-cradle is for small babies.  Side-lying reduces pressure on the hematoma.

questions.questionText: Newborn has hyperbilirubinemia and prescribed phototherapy.  Which intervention should nurse do?
Pregnant client.  Which should they report immediately?
questions.explanationText: Swelling of face and fingers is an indiation fo HTN disorders such as eclampsia.

questions.questionText: Pregnant client.  35 weeks gestation has preeclampsia without severe features.  Which is a priority?
questions.explanationText: Minimal accepted level is 30 mL/hr.  +2 edema of the feet is expected at 35 weeks gestation.  +1 protein in urine and blood pressure of 144/92 mmHg is nonurgent because it is an expected finding for client with preeclampsia.

questions.questionText: A nurse is preparing to perform a fundal assessment on a postpartum client. The initial nursing action in performing this assessment is which of the following?
options: Ask the client to turn on her side
Ask the client to lie flat on her back with the knees and legs flat and straight.
Ask the mother to urinate and empty her bladder
Massage the fundus gently before determining the level of the fundus.
questions.correctAnswer: Ask the mother to urinate and empty her bladder

questions.questionText: The nurse is assessing the lochia on a 1 day PP patient. The nurse notes that the lochia is red and has a foul-smelling odor. The nurse determines that this assessment finding is:
options: Indicates the need for increasing oral fluids
Indicates the need for increasing ambulation

questions.questionText: Which are a risk factor for postpartum hemorrhage
options: Twins; Fourth Pregnancy; Previous cesarean section
questions.correctAnswer: Twins; Fourth Pregnancy; Previous cesarean section

questions.questionText: The nurse is assessing the incision of a postpartum patient who had a cesarean section. Which of the following would the nurse assess?
questions.explanationText: Know REEDA!!!

questions.questionText: Nurse caring for postpartum client understands that breast engorgement is caused by
options: Increased blood supply to the breasts
questions.correctAnswer: Increased blood supply to the breasts
questions.explanationText: primary engorgement is increased blood supply

questions.questionText: Which of the following statements indicate that the postpartum client understands the baby blues?
options: I may experience detachment and lack of bonding with the baby
Excessive crying and anxiety are signs of the baby blues
The baby blues can last up to six weeks
questions.correctAnswer: Excessive crying and anxiety are signs of the baby blues
questions.explanationText: Baby blues lasts 2 weeks.  Detachment/lack of bonding is ALWAYS a red flag

questions.questionText: What is the priority nursing intervention when a postpartum client has excessive vaginal bleeding?
questions.explanationText: Fundal massage stimulates the uterus to contract

questions.questionText: A steady trickle of bright red blood from the vagina in the presence of a firm uterine fundus suggests
questions.explanationText: Know the 4 T's of bleeding.  Tone, Tissue, Trauma, Thrombin.  If it is firm, it is not uterine actony (non contracted uterus), so it is then lacerations of genital tract

questions.questionText: a
a
a
